prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "站立侧抬腿" (standing side leg raise) exercise, front view, facing the viewer. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a standing side leg raise: right leg supporting with foot flat on the ground and knee almost fully extended (slight bend); left leg fully extended straight out to the side at about 30-45 degrees away from the body, foot flexed (toes pointing forward); torso upright with neutral spine, hands on hips for support; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus medius of the lifted (left) hip. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and core muscles for stability. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀中肌" pointing to the gluteus medius of the lifted hip; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "核心肌群" pointing to the core muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
